game_description: Roll dice for points â€” but farkle (score nothing) and you lose your turn.

strategies.strategy_detailed: Must reach 500+ in one turn to enter. Stopping below 500 scores zero â€” same as farkling, no carryover. Push through 200â€“400 sequences to hit 500 even at moderate farkle risk. The rolling table does not apply until you are on the board.
strategy_stealth: Need 500 in one turn to enter â€” below = zero
Push through 200â€“400 to qualify
Rolling table starts after first bank

strategies.strategy_detailed: Bank when turn total meets threshold for remaining dice: 6â†’always roll, 5â†’bank â‰Ą3000, 4â†’bank â‰Ą1050, 3â†’bank â‰Ą450, 2â†’bank â‰Ą250, 1 dieâ†’bank â‰Ą350. 1-die threshold exceeds 2-dice (350 vs 250) because 1 die has 67% farkle rate. Farkle rates: 1=67%, 2=44%, 3=28%, 4=16%, 5=8%, 6=2%.
strategy_stealth: 6 diceâ†’always roll | 5 diceâ†’bank â‰Ą3000
4 diceâ†’bank â‰Ą1050 | 3 diceâ†’bank â‰Ą450
2 diceâ†’bank â‰Ą250 | 1 dieâ†’bank â‰Ą350

strategies.strategy_detailed: Don't maximize points scored â€” maximize expected turn total. Check each option's resulting (points, dice) against the rolling table. If max points puts you above bank threshold with few dice left, consider taking fewer to stay rolling. Ties: prefer more dice remaining. Always take points that win.
strategy_stealth: Stay below bank threshold to keep rolling
More dice remaining > more points scored
Always take points that win the game

strategies.strategy_detailed: When all 6 dice score, reroll all 6 with accumulated points safe. Always reroll on hot dice â€” EV is strongly positive at every turn total. Hot dice chains are the primary path to 1000+ turn scores. Passing on hot dice is the most common Farkle mistake.
strategy_stealth: All 6 dice score â†’ reroll all 6, no exception
EV positive at all turn totals â€” never skip
Hot dice chains â†’ 1000+ turn scores

strategies.condition: Deciding turn target in normal game state
strategies.strategy_detailed: Target 500â€“800 per turn using the rolling table as guide. Turns above 1000 almost always come from hot dice chains, not from pushing past thresholds. Accept a bank at 450â€“600 and start fresh rather than squeezing one more risky roll.
strategy_stealth: Target 500â€“800 per turn
1000+ turns come from hot dice chains
Accept a good bank â€” start next turn fresh

strategies.strategy_detailed: Rolling table thresholds collapse when behind. With a 2000-point deficit: 3 diceâ†’bank â‰Ą600 (vs 450 base), 4 diceâ†’bank â‰Ą1750 (vs 1050 base). A conservative 500-point turn when you need 2000 to catch up is nearly worthless. Accept farkle risk â€” falling further behind loses the game.
strategy_stealth: Deficit 2000+: thresholds roughly double
3 diceâ†’â‰Ą600 | 4 diceâ†’â‰Ą1750
Small safe turns lose â€” swing or fall behind

strategies.strategy_detailed: Three-of-a-kind: triple 1s=1000, others=100Ă— face (2s=200, 3s=300, 4s=400, 5s=500, 6s=600). Each extra die doubles: four 2s=400, five 2s=800, six 2s=1600. Straights and three pairs each score 1500. If all 6 dice score (e.g., two triplets), it is hot dice â€” reroll all 6.
strategy_stealth: Triple 1s=1000 | others=100Ă— face
Each extra die in set doubles value
All 6 dice score = hot dice, reroll all

strategies.condition: 3 or more players in the game
strategies.strategy_detailed: In winner-take-all multiplayer, EV-optimal play underperforms. With 4 players, one lucky aggressive player beats three careful ones. Treat all rolling thresholds ~25% higher. More farkles is acceptable â€” you need big turns to win, not consistent average turns.
strategy_stealth: EV play loses to high-variance in 3+ players
Treat all thresholds ~25% higher
More farkles OK â€” big turns win games

strategies.strategy_detailed: Ignore the rolling table when either player is within ~1000 of winning. If you are close: roll until you win or farkle â€” banks that don't win are nearly worthless. If opponent is close and you are far back: swing for a huge turn. Endgame is binary: win now or fight to deny them.
strategy_stealth: 9000+: ignore rolling table entirely
Banks that don't win = nearly worthless
Roll until win threshold or farkle

strategies.strategy_detailed: All remaining players get one final turn. Chasing: within 500 pts play normally; 1500+ behind, roll until you pass their score or farkle. Leading after hitting 10000: maximize before banking â€” 10400 is far safer than 10050. Every 100-point margin reduces opponents' steal chance.
strategy_stealth: Everyone gets 1 final turn at 10000
Chasing 1500+: roll until pass or farkle
Leading: maximize â€” 10400 >> 10050
